channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Phasmophobia Player Character Update Brings More Customization Next Week
item.content:encoded:
Kinetic Games has revealed the release date for the upcoming Phasmophobia Player Character update, a long-awaited patch that will allow you to customize your character with greater depth and expression.

First teased as part of the game's 2026 roadmap back in January, the Player Character update is arriving on May 5th on all platforms. It'll bring an overhaul for character design, as well as tons of new cosmetics, which you'll be able to obtain "microtransaction-free", according to Kinetic.

Additionally, you can look forward to "new hairstyles, accessories, innerwear, outerwear, and more", so if you've ever wanted to express yourself more thoroughly while sprinting for the door because another ghost has ruined your amygdala, now's your chance.

As well as new cosmetics, you'll also be treated to brand new animations and movements for character models, so hopefully, you and your fellow ghost-hunters should look and feel just that little bit more realistic after the update drops.

Every cosmetic item in Phasmophobia will apparently be unlockable either through in-game challenges or by earning in-game currency and buying what you want through the store with that currency. You won't have to spend a single real dollar.

The update won't just include cosmetic overhauls, though; it'll also bring quality-of-life improvements like revamped death and revival animations, as well as lots of other bug fixes and performance tweaks.

News of the latest Phasmophobia update comes just over a week after Kinetic announced a crossover between its ghost-hunting horror game and Remedy's mind-bending Alan Wake 2. A movie adaptation of Kinetic's game is also in the works at horror production studio Blumhouse.

Phasmophobia's Player Character update arrives across PC and consoles on May 5th. The game is available to check out right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S, so give it a look if you and your buddies are after some scares.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: GreedFall and Steelrising Dev Spiders Reportedly Shutting Down
item.content:encoded:
Spiders, the studio responsible for RPGs like GreedFall and The Technomancer, is apparently shutting down following parent company Nacon's reported failure to find a buyer.

According to French gaming news platform Origami (via ResetEra user GodGraviel; we're using machine translation for the original article), Nacon hasn't managed to find any interested parties to purchase Spiders, and so the publisher's administrator will request liquidation tomorrow.

As a reminder, Spiders was one of the studios that filed for insolvency alongside Nacon back in March, with the publisher's decision coming just days after the announcement of the latest Nacon Connect presentation (although that presentation was subsequently delayed to May).

The writing may have been on the wall for Spiders for some time, unfortunately. Despite a strike that secured better pay and work policies for the studio's staff in late 2024, Spiders became one of the many developers hit by layoffs last year, with "most of the animation and rig team" losing their jobs.

Sadly, it seems that RPG sequel GreedFall: The Dying World failed to find the audience Spiders desperately needed in order to make its future less uncertain. At its peak, the game managed to attract just under 1,000 Steam players, and just 88 players have checked GreedFall 2 out in the last 24 hours.

Steam player numbers naturally don't tell the whole story, especially when it comes to single-player games that don't rely on player retention for continued revenue, but that's still an alarmingly low number of people taking a look at Spiders' latest.

Spiders is not the only studio to shut down in recent months. It joins the likes of G.I. Joe Snake Eyes developer Atomic Arcade, the Halifax branch of Ubisoft, and Squid Game: Unleashed studio Boss Fight, among others.

If you'd like to memorialize Spiders, you can check out the studio's latest game, GreedFall: The Dying World, right now on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/greedfall-steelrising-spiders-shutting-down-report
